title: SNA Declares Baidoa Fully Secured; Remaining SW State Forces Scattered
detail: Somali National Army forces declared Baidoa fully secured on March 31, with remaining Southwest State loyalist security forces confined to scattered pockets. The swift military operation — taking less than 48 hours — effectively ended the constitutional standoff between the Federal Government of Somalia and Southwest State. President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's government moved to assert direct federal authority over the city and Southwest State administration. The operation was presented by Mogadishu as a law enforcement and constitutional order action, while critics — including opposition MPs, Jubbaland, and Puntland — condemned it as an unconstitutional military coup against a democratically re-elected state president.

title: Puntland Withdraws Recognition of Federal Government, Declares Independent Operation
detail: Puntland State announced it was withdrawing recognition of the Federal Government of Somalia and would operate independently until a new constitution acceptable to all federal member states — subject to a public referendum — is agreed. The announcement by Puntland's administration under President Said Abdullahi Deni followed the SNA seizure of Baidoa and Laftagareen's resignation. Puntland had already been in a stand-off with Mogadishu over the March 2026 constitutional amendments. The move marks the most serious rupture in Puntland-FGS relations since Puntland declared itself a semi-autonomous state in 1998. Somalia now faces a genuine governance fracture: the FGS controls Mogadishu and the newly seized Baidoa but faces open non-recognition from Puntland and intense hostility from Jubbaland. The constitutional crisis is compounded by the approaching expiry of parliamentary mandates (April 14) and presidential terms (May 15) under the 2012 Provisional Constitution.

media.caption: Puntland withdraws recognition of federal government, declares independent operation
